claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: This is still a factor-level computational-basis action model, not a complex linear operator, Hamiltonian sum, Hermiticity proof, spectral theorem, Quantum PCP/NLTS theorem, global impossibility result, BQP separation, or quantum-advantage claim.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Lean checks that two Pauli factors on distinct Fin n sites commute under the restricted computational-basis replay, with matching final basis state and phase projections.

command: mkdir -p .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/WidthLocality.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/WidthLocality.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliBasisAction.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliBasisAction.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionComposition.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionComposition.lean && lake env lean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionCommutation.lean

requirements.label: Lean and Lake version probes return zero
All four B9 modules compile together
The helper proves double flips commute at distinct sites
The phase alphabet has a checked commutative multiplication lemma
Disjoint Pauli factor actions commute as composed actions
The state projection of disjoint replay is order independent
The phase projection of disjoint replay is order independent
The proof imports the prior compositional replay contract
The checked module contains no matrix or complex-amplitude machinery
Fresh transcript binds all source and project hashes
The source keeps the restricted proof boundary explicit
